title: [lora_l2.c:412] Error backoff sleep is not interruptible by state change
description:
Gap: The 1000ms k_sleep in the error backoff path cannot be interrupted by stop().

What: Line 412 uses k_sleep(K_MSEC(1000)) for backoff on consecutive errors. Unlike lora_recv() which uses a timeout, k_sleep cannot be interrupted by the thread checking its state. If stop() is called during this sleep, the thread won't exit until the sleep completes.

Why it matters: Already documented in the comments (project-LICHEN-i1gk.103) but the interaction is subtle: if CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2_RX_TIMEOUT_MS < 1000ms, the stop() join timeout may expire during this backoff, causing forced thread abort. This is an edge case where aggressive Kconfig tuning can degrade graceful shutdown to forced abort.
close_reason: Already documented: The 1000ms backoff sleep has extensive documentation (lines 438-454) explaining the interaction with RX_THREAD_QUICK_JOIN_MS, why forced abort is acceptable for persistent hardware errors, and that recovery requires reinitialization anyway. Referenced as project-LICHEN-i1gk.103. The tradeoffs are explicitly acknowledged; no change needed.
